Low-Level UDN Support (arch/udn.h)
[Tile Architecture Headers]

Detailed Description

Low level support for the UDN (user dynamic network).

Simple packets can be sent between cores over the UDN.

The UDN offers four demultiplexing queues (demuxes). Packets of data can be sent from one core to a specified demux on another core.

To send a packet on the UDN, use udn_send() to send a special header word (encoding the destination tile; the destination demux; and the total number of words in the packet, not including the header word itself), and then the "payload" words.

Use fill_dynamic_header to construct the special header word.

To receive a packet on one of the four demuxes, use udn0_receive() (and the like) to receive each word of the "payload". Note that the header and the tag are handled automatically and are NOT seen by udn0_receive().

The UDN offers four demux queues; a separate function is used with each one (see udn0_receive(), udn1_available_count(), and similar).

Data Structures

union  DynamicHeader
 Final routing destinations for dynamic messages. More...

Defines

#define UDN_DEMUX_WORDS   (249 + 4)
 The size of the UDN demux, in 8-byte words.
#define __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(n)   (n)
 UDN register numbers are used literally as tags.
#define UDN0_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(0)
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 0.
#define UDN1_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(1)
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 1.
#define UDN2_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(2)
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 2.
#define UDN3_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(3)
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 3.

Functions

unsigned int INIT_DYNAMIC_HEADER (int x, int y, int demux, int length)
 Initialize a DynamicHeader option with specific values.
unsigned int fill_dynamic_header (int x, int y, int demux, int length)
 Generate a dynamic header.
unsigned int udn_tile_coord (void)
 Get the UDN tile coordinate.
unsigned int udn_tile_coord_x (void)
 Get the X component of the UDN tile coordinate.
unsigned int udn_tile_coord_y (void)
 Get the Y component of the UDN tile coordinate.
void udn_send (uint_reg_t word)
 Send a word onto the UDN.
uint_reg_t udn0_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 0.
uint_reg_t udn1_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 1.
uint_reg_t udn2_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 2.
uint_reg_t udn3_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 3.
unsigned int udn0_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 0.
unsigned int udn1_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 1.
unsigned int udn2_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 2.
unsigned int udn3_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 3.
unsigned int udn_available_mask (void)
 Determine which UDN demuxes have data available.

Define Documentation

#define UDN_DEMUX_WORDS   (249 + 4)

The size of the UDN demux, in 8-byte words.

#define __UDN_REG_TO_TAG ( n   )     (n)

UDN register numbers are used literally as tags.

#define UDN0_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(0)

The default tag for UDN demux queue 0.

Examples:
tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c.

#define UDN1_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(1)

The default tag for UDN demux queue 1.

#define UDN2_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(2)

The default tag for UDN demux queue 2.

#define UDN3_DEMUX_TAG   __UDN_REG_TO_TAG(3)

The default tag for UDN demux queue 3.

Function Documentation

unsigned int INIT_DYNAMIC_HEADER ( int  x,
int  y,
int  demux,
int  length 
)

Initialize a DynamicHeader option with specific values.

This macro simplifies initializing a header. Use it like:

DynamicHeader hdr = INIT_DYNAMIC_HEADER(1,2,0,16);

unsigned int fill_dynamic_header ( int  x,
int  y,
int  demux,
int  length 
)

Generate a dynamic header.

Fills in a word-sized dynamic header with specified routing parameters.

Parameters:
x the x coordinate of the destination tile
y the y coordinate of the destination tile
demux the demux queue to which the packet will be delivered at the destination tile
length how many words will be sent after the header. This value must be greater than 0 and less than 128.

unsigned int udn_tile_coord ( void   ) 

Get the UDN tile coordinate.

unsigned int udn_tile_coord_x ( void   ) 

Get the X component of the UDN tile coordinate.

unsigned int udn_tile_coord_y ( void   ) 

Get the Y component of the UDN tile coordinate.

void udn_send ( uint_reg_t  word  ) 

Send a word onto the UDN.

Parameters:
word The word to be sent.

uint_reg_t udn0_receive ( void   ) 

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 0.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

uint_reg_t udn1_receive ( void   ) 

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 1.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

uint_reg_t udn2_receive ( void   ) 

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 2.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

uint_reg_t udn3_receive ( void   ) 

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 3.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

unsigned int udn0_available_count ( void   ) 

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 0.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

unsigned int udn1_available_count ( void   ) 

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 1.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

unsigned int udn2_available_count ( void   ) 

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 2.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

unsigned int udn3_available_count ( void   ) 

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 3.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

unsigned int udn_available_mask ( void   ) 

Determine which UDN demuxes have data available.

Returns:
A mask of "SPR_UDN_DATA_AVAIL__AVAIL_*_MASK" values, based on which UDN demuxes currently have data available.
